imu-selection.description: Guida completa ad selectionem MEMS IMU: Explanatio detaillata de stabilitate bias gyrometri, ambulatione random angularis, latitudine bandi et aliis paramertris criticis, adiuvans vos ad eligendum unitatem mensurae inertialium maxime accommodatum
imu-selection.title: Guida Completa ad Selectionem MEMS IMU: Quomodo Eligere Unitatem Mensurae Inertialis Appriopriatam | MEMS-MCTI Blogum Technicum
imu-selection.keywords: Selectio MEMS IMU, Parametri Unitatis Mensurae Inertialis, Stabilitas Bias Gyrometri, Selectio Accelerometri, Sensorium MEMS
imu-selection.og_title: Guida Completa ad Selectionem MEMS IMU: Quomodo Eligere Unitatem Mensurae Inertialis Appriopriatam
imu-selection.og_description: Explanatio detaillata de stabilitate bias gyrometri, ambulatione random angularis, latitudine bandi et aliis paramertris criticis, adiuvans vos ad eligendum unitatem mensurae inertialium maxime accommodatum
imu-selection.h1: Guida Completa ad Selectionem MEMS IMU: Quomodo Eligere Unitatem Mensurae Inertialis Appriopriatam
imu-selection.intro: Unitas Mensurae Inertialis (IMU) est sensor primarius systematum navigationis modernae, late usitata in UAV, aviatione, spatio, mari navigatione, gubernatione automata et automatione industriali. Hic articulus introducet paramertris technicos claves considerandos in selectione MEMS IMU, adiuvans ingeniarios ad eligendum productum maxime appropriatum secundum necessitates applicationis specificae.
imu-selection.p1_1: IMU MEMS typicale continet duos sensores centrales:
imu-selection.li1_1: Gyrometrum trium axium: Mensurat velocitatem angularum circa tres axes orthogonales (°/s)
imu-selection.li1_2: Accelerometrum trium axium: Mensurat accelerationem linearum secundum tres axes orthogonales (m/s² vel g)
imu-selection.p1_2: Quaedam IMU altae qualitatis etiam possunt integrare magnetometrum trium axium ad praebendum reference heading absoluti.
imu-selection.p2_1: Gamut mensurae indicat maximam velocitatem angularum quam gyrometrum metiri potest. Specificationes communes sunt ±300°/s ad ±1000°/s. In selectione oportet considerare maximam velocitatem angularum vehiculi:
imu-selection.p2_2: Stabilitas bias est maxime cardo indicatorum performantiae IMU, indicans gradum deviationis stochasticorum output signi gyrometri in statu quiescente, de norma °/h (gradus per horam). Hic parametrum directe determinat praecisionem longi temporis in solutione attitude.
imu-selection.p2_3: Stabilitas bias gyrometri PA-IMU-01D est minus quam 0.5°/h, significans errorem attitude non excedentem 0.5 gradus per horam.
imu-selection.h3_3: 3. Ambulatio Random Angularis (Angular Random Walk, ARW)
imu-selection.p2_4: Ambulatio random angularis est effectus integrationis noise albidi output gyrometri, de norma °/√h. Describit rationem crescentis erroris estimationis angularis gyrometri cum tempore. Pro applicationibus quae responsum dynamicum rapidum desiderant, hic parametrum est magni momenti.
imu-selection.p2_5: Latitudo bandi indicat range frequentiae intra quam output gyrometri potest accurate sequi variationem input velocitatis angularis, in Hz. PA-IMU-01D habet latitudinem bandi maiorem quam 100Hz, quae potest satisfacere necessitate responsi dynamici plurimarum systematum gubernationis. Pro UAV volanti alte speed vel armis gubernatis, maior latitudo bandi necessaria est.
imu-selection.p2_6: Non-linearitas factoris scalae indicat gradum deviationis relationis linearis inter output et input velocitatis angularis gyrometri, de norma ppm (partes per million). Non-linearitas factoris scalae PA-IMU-01D est minus quam 100ppm, garantizans altam praecisionem mensurae in toto range.
imu-selection.p3_1: Gamut mensurae accelerometri de norma in g (acceleratio gravitationis). Gamut mensurae accelerometri PA-IMU-01D est ±10g, potens sustinere magnos impetus et vibrationes. Pro mensuratione micro-vibrationum ut in ponte detectione, minor gamut (ut ±2g) necessarius est ad obtinendam maiorum resolutionem.
imu-selection.p3_2: Stabilitas bias accelerometri determinat praecisionem mensurae accelerationis in statu quiescente. Stabilitas bias accelerometri PA-IMU-01D est minus quam 1mg (milligrammum), id est potens discernere variationem accelerationis 0.001g.
imu-selection.p3_3: Resolutio est minima variatio accelerationis quam accelerometrum discernere potest. Alta resolutio est maximi momenti pro applicationibus mensurationis precisi ut monitoringus sanitatis structurae et seismicus monitoringus.
imu-selection.env1: Temperatura Operationis: PA-IMU-01D sustinet range temperaturae operationis -40°C ad +60°C
imu-selection.env2: Resistentia Impactus: Designatio altae resistentiae ad impaktum, potens sustinere impaktum 1000g
imu-selection.env3: Resistentia Vibrationis: Output stabile in environmento vibrationis fortis
imu-selection.iface1: RS-422: Longa distantia, fortis resistentia ad interference, conveniens pro loco industriali
imu-selection.iface2: RS-232: Protocolum commune serialis portus, bona compatibilitas
imu-selection.iface3: CAN: Standard automobile et industriali gubernationis, sustinet communicationem alti speed
imu-selection.summary: Selectio MEMS IMU appropriati oportet considering multiplices factores ut requisita praecisionis, condiciones environmenti, genera interface et budget. Series PA-IMU praebet lineam productorum completam a gradu consumer ad gradum industriadem, potens satisfacere necessitates plurimarum occasionum applicationum.
imu-selection.contact: Pro ulteriori consultatione selectionis, welcome contactare team supporti technici nostri.
imu-selection.related1: IMU, AHRS et GPS-INS: Quod Systema Navigationis Tibi Necessarium Est?
imu-selection.related2: Analysis Principii RTK GPS: Realizatio Positionis Centimetri-gradus

imu-vs-ahrs.title: IMU contra AHRS contra GPS-INS: Quod Systema Navigationis Tibi Necessarium Est? | MEMS-MCTI Blogum Technicum
imu-vs-ahrs.description: Comparatio profunda IMU, AHRS et GPS-INS: principia operativa, characteristiculae et occasus applicationis, adiuvans ingeniarios ad eligendum correctum solutionem navigationis
imu-vs-ahrs.keywords: IMU contra AHRS, GPS-INS navigatio inertialium, selectio systematis navigationis, systema attitude, navigatio integrata
imu-vs-ahrs.og_title: IMU contra AHRS contra GPS-INS: Quod Systema Navigationis Tibi Necessarium Est?
imu-vs-ahrs.og_description: Comparatio profunda IMU, AHRS et GPS-INS: principia operativa, characteristiculae et occasus applicationis
imu-vs-ahrs.h1: IMU contra AHRS contra GPS-INS: Quod Systema Navigationis Tibi Necessarium Est?
imu-vs-ahrs.intro: In selectione systematis navigationis, ingeniarii saepe observant selectionem inter IMU, AHRS et GPS-INS. Unumquodque habet suas characteristiculas et convenit pro diversis occasibus applicationis. Hic articulus continet analysim profundam principiorum operativorum, utilitatum, incommodorum et occasus applicationis horum trium systematum, qui adiuvabit vos ad faciendum selectionem rectam.
imu-vs-ahrs.p1_1: IMU est unitas sensorum fundamentalis systematis navigationis. Functio eius principalis est mensurare velocitatem angularum et accelerationem linearem vehiculi. Praebet datos crusor sensorum non processatos:
imu-vs-ahrs.li1_1: Velocitas angularum trium axium (ωx, ωy, ωz)
imu-vs-ahrs.li1_2: Acceleratio linearum trium axium (ax, ay, az)
imu-vs-ahrs.p1_2: IMU per se non calculat attitude; processor externus requiritur ad convertendos datos crusores in angulos attitude (pitch, roll, yaw).
imu-vs-ahrs.p2_1: AHRS, fundamentatum in IMU, est instructum cum magnetometro et/sive GPS, etiam cum algoritmo interno fusionis datos attitude, permittens directam praebitionem:
imu-vs-ahrs.p2_2: PA-AHRS01 etiam est instructum cum receptor GPS, permittens praebitionem informationum latitudinis et longitudinis.
imu-vs-ahrs.p3_1: GPS-INS est systema navigationis maxime progressum, profundo combinans GPS (vel alias systemas navigationis satellites) cum INS (Systema Navigationis Inertialis), permittens praebitionem:
imu-vs-ahrs.li_pros1: Alta frequentia renovationis datorum (usque ad 1000 Hz)
imu-vs-ahrs.li_pros5: Convenit pro integratione profunda cum systematis gubernationis volandi
imu-vs-ahrs.li_cons2: Error attitude accumulat drift cum tempore
imu-vs-ahrs.li_cons3: Nulla referentia heading absoluta (auxilium magnetometri requiritur)
imu-vs-ahrs.li_cons4: Frequentia output plerumque est inferior quam IMU originalis
imu-vs-ahrs.li_pros7: GPS et INS se complementant, praecisio altissima
imu-vs-ahrs.li_pros8: INS sustinet altam praecisionem in defectu GPS
imu-vs-ahrs.li_pros9: Supportum RTK permittit obtinere praecisionem centimetri-gradus positionis
imu-vs-ahrs.li_scen1: Systemata Gubernationis Volandi UAV: datos sensorum originales gubernationi volandi requiruntur pro filtratione et fusione customi
imu-vs-ahrs.li_scen2: Gubernatio Attitude Robotis: alti-speed feedback attitude cum latentia humili requiritur
imu-vs-ahrs.li_scen3: Platformae Stabilizationis Industriales: integratio profunda cum systematis gubernationis extantibus requiritur
imu-vs-ahrs.li_scen4: Instituta Investigationis: algorithmi navigationis proprietarii evolvendi sunt
imu-vs-ahrs.li_scen5: Producta UAV Parata: parata ad usum, cyclus evolutionis breviatus
imu-vs-ahrs.li_scen6: Monitoringus Attitude Vehiculi: anguli attitude directi legendi requiruntur
imu-vs-ahrs.li_scen7: Monitoringus Attitude Navis: requisita praecisionis attitude certa sunt
imu-vs-ahrs.li_scen9: Navigatio Aviatica: informatio completa positionis et attitude requiritur
imu-vs-ahrs.li_scen10: Mensura Altae Praecisionis: praecisio centimetri-gradus in modo RTK
imu-vs-ahrs.li_scen11: Gubernatio Automatum: positus et attitude altae praecisionis requiruntur
imu-vs-ahrs.li_scen12: Agriculture Precisa: records locationis precisi operationum requiruntur
imu-vs-ahrs.desc1: IMU praebet frequentiam renovationis usque ad 1000Hz
imu-vs-ahrs.desc2: Output IMU habet latentiam valde humilem
imu-vs-ahrs.desc3: Optima pro integratione cum gubernatione volandi
imu-vs-ahrs.summary: Haec tria systemata navigationis deserviunt diversis necessitatibus applicationis:
imu-vs-ahrs.li_sum1: IMU: convenit pro applicationibus quae configurationem profundam et gubernationem desiderant
imu-vs-ahrs.li_sum2: AHRS: convenit pro productis quae output attitude paratum desiderant
imu-vs-ahrs.li_sum3: GPS-INS: convenit pro applicationibus excelsis quae solutiones navigationis completas desiderant
imu-vs-ahrs.contact: In selectione oportet considerare requisita praecisionis, limitamina budgeti, cyclum evolutionis et capacitatem technicam. Pro consultationibus ulterioribus, quaeso contactate supportum technicum nostrum.
imu-vs-ahrs.related1: Guida Completa ad Selectionem MEMS IMU

rtk-gps.title: Principia Operativa RTK GPS: Technologiae Claves ad Obtinendum Praecisionem Centimetri-Gradus Positionis | MEMS-MCTI Blogum Technicum
rtk-gps.description: Analysis principiorum operativorum RTK GPS: descriptio detaillata technologiarum claudium ut mensura phasis carrieris, correctio differentialis, resolutioambiguitatis et aliae, ad obtinendum praecisionem centimetri-gradus positionis
rtk-gps.keywords: Principia Operativa RTK GPS, positio phasis carrieris, GPS differentialis, praecisio centimetri-gradus positionis, praecisio positionis GPS, mensura RTK
rtk-gps.og_title: Principia Operativa RTK GPS: Technologiae Claves ad Obtinendum Praecisionem Centimetri-Gradus Positionis
rtk-gps.og_description: Descriptio detaillata technologiarum claudium RTK: mensura phasis carrieris, correctio differentialis, resolutio ambiguitatis et aliae
rtk-gps.h1: Principia Operativa RTK GPS: Technologiae Claves ad Obtinendum Praecisionem Centimetri-Gradus Positionis
rtk-gps.intro: RTK (Cinematica Temporis Realis) est technologia positionis GPS altae praecisionis, permittens obtinere praecisionem centimetri-gradus positionis in tempore reali. In hoc articulo principia operativorum RTK, technologiae claudium et occasus applicationis describerentur.
rtk-gps.p1_1: Satellitess GPS transmittunt duo genera signorum distantiae:
rtk-gps.li1_1: Carrier L1 (1575.42 MHz): carrier codicis C/A et codicis P
rtk-gps.li1_2: Carrier L2 (1227.60 MHz): solus codex P (cryptatus ut codex Y)
rtk-gps.p1_2: Longitudo undae carrieris: L1 circa 19.4 cm, L2 circa 24.4 cm. Praecisio mensurae phasis carrieris est plus quam 100-plo praecisionem mensurae pseudodistantiae codicis.
rtk-gps.p2_1: Positionatio GPS traditionalis computat positionem per mensurationem pseudodistantiae a satelliti ad receptorem per triangulationem. Pseudodistantia = tempus propagationis signi × velocitas lucis.
rtk-gps.p2_2: Attamen praecisio mensurae pseudodistantiae est limitata (circa 0.5–3 metra). Fontes principales errorum includunt:
rtk-gps.p3_1: Mensura phasis carrieris utitur informatione phasis carrierum L1/L2 ad computandum distantiam. Receptor mensurat differentiam phasium inter signum carrieris receptum et signum referentiae internum.
rtk-gps.p3_2: Mensura phasis carrieris potest exprimi ut:
rtk-gps.p3_3: Ubi N est ambiguitas integralis (ambiguitas integri incogniti), quae est paramum clavus non resolutus in mensura phasis carrieris.
rtk-gps.p4_1: Ambiguitas integralis N est numerus cycludorum integrorum carrieris inter receptor et satellitem. Cum N est определеноiste accurat determination, potest computare distantiam valde accuratam per phasim carrieris.
rtk-gps.li4_1: Methodus Quaerendi: constructio regionis candidatorum ambiguitatis, inventio solutionis optimae per methodum minimorum quadratorum
rtk-gps.li4_2: LAMBDA: methodus minimorum quadratorum cum reductione correlationis ambiguitatis, maxime late usa in industria
rtk-gps.li4_3: Filtrum Kalman: aestimatio dynamica ambiguitatis et variationum eius
rtk-gps.p5_1: DGPS elevat praecisionem positionis per mensurationem errorum GPS in statione basi cum positione nota et mittendo has correctiones ad rover.
rtk-gps.p5_3: Post applicationem correctionum a rover, praecisio potest elevari ad 1–5 metra.
rtk-gps.p6_1: RTK facit adhuc unum passum ultra DGPS, utens phasi carrieris ut mensura:
rtk-gps.li6_1: Statio basi: positus notus accurate, continuus sectatio phasium carrierum omnium satellitum visibilium
rtk-gps.li6_2: Rover: simul observat phasess carrierum satellitum et accipit datos correctionum a statione basi
rtk-gps.li6_3: Correctio in tempore reali: transmissio correctionum phasis carrieris in tempore reali per canalem radio
rtk-gps.p6_2: Clavis RTK est quod statio basi et rover simul observant eosdem satellites, errores habent correlationem altam, et per differentiam duplicem observationum, pluresque errores communes eliminari possunt.
rtk-gps.p7_1: RTK utitur differentia duplici (DD) observationum ad eliminandum errores systemicos:
rtk-gps.li7_1: Differentia simplex: differentia observationum unius satellitis inter rover et stationem basi, eliminat errorem horologii satellitis
rtk-gps.li7_2: Differentia duplex: differentia differentiarum simplicum inter duos satellites, eliminat errorem horologii receptoris
rtk-gps.p7_2: Post tractationem differentiae duplex, errores principales residui sunt:
rtk-gps.li7_3: Latentia residualis in atmosphaera (negligibilis pro basi brevi <5 km)
rtk-gps.li_uav1: Mensura punctorum referentiae pro photography aeriali altae praecisionis
rtk-gps.h2_7: VII. Recommendationes Productorum Seriei PA-GS RTK
rtk-gps.summary: Technologia RTK obtinet praecisionem centimetri-gradus positionis in tempore reali per tres elementos clavigeros: mensuram phasis carrieris, correctionem differentialem in tempore reali, et resolutionem ambiguitatis integrationis. Producta seriei PA-GS RTK combinant integrationem multisystematicam (GPS/GLONASS/BDS) et emissionem datorum cum alta frequentia renovationis, praebentes solutions firmas pro mensura, agricultura precisa, UAV et gubernatione operum ingeniariorum.
rtk-gps.contact: Pro ulteriori informatione de productis vel consultationibus technicis, quaeso contactate team venditionis nostrum.
rtk-gps.product1Desc: Systema Orientationis RTK GPS cum Duo Antennis
rtk-gps.related1: Guida Completa ad Selectionem MEMS IMU

uav-imu.title: UAV IMU Selection Guide: How to Choose the Right IMU for Your Aircraft | MEMS-MCTI Tech Blog
uav-imu.description: UAV IMU Selection Guide: Detailed explanation of key parameters like dynamic response, vibration damping, sampling rate for multi-rotor, fixed-wing, and helicopter applications
uav-imu.keywords: UAV flight control, IMU selection, multi-rotor, fixed-wing, attitude control, MEMS IMU
uav-imu.og_title: UAV IMU Selection Guide: How to Choose the Right IMU for Your Aircraft
uav-imu.og_description: Detailed explanation of key parameters like dynamic response, vibration damping, sampling rate for UAV flight control IMU
uav-imu.h1: UAV IMU Selection Guide: How to Choose the Right Inertial Measurement Unit for Your Aircraft
uav-imu.intro: The flight control system of UAVs is core to ensuring flight stability and safety, and the IMU is the key sensor of the flight control system. This article details the working principles, key parameters and selection suggestions for UAV flight control IMUs to help you choose the most suitable IMU for multi-rotor, fixed-wing or helicopter applications.
uav-imu.h3_1: 1. Role of IMU in Flight Control
uav-imu.p1_1: UAV flight control relies on real-time perception and precise control of aircraft attitude. The flight control system obtains angular velocity and acceleration data through the IMU, calculates required control commands using control algorithms, and achieves stable control of flight attitude.
uav-imu.li1_1: Attitude angles (Roll, Pitch, Yaw): Calculated by integrating gyroscope data
uav-imu.li1_2: Angular velocity: Real-time measurement of body rotation rate around three axes
uav-imu.li1_3: Acceleration: Measures linear acceleration of the body, used for detecting maneuvers
uav-imu.li1_4: Vibration monitoring: Detects vibration status during flight for vibration suppression
uav-imu.p1_2: A typical UAV flight control system includes the following components:
uav-imu.p2_1: UAVs experience rapid attitude changes during flight, and IMUs must have excellent dynamic response capability:
uav-imu.li2_1: Measurement range: ±300°/s~±500°/s recommended for multi-rotor, ±1000°/s+ for high-speed fixed-wing
uav-imu.li2_2: Bandwidth: >100Hz recommended to ensure fidelity during rapid maneuvers
uav-imu.li2_3: Sampling rate: Flight control systems typically require >200Hz raw data update rate
uav-imu.p2_2: UAVs, especially multi-rotor aircraft, generate significant vibration from motors and propellers. IMU vibration damping capability directly affects flight control performance:
uav-imu.li2_4: Mechanical isolation: Use shock mounts to isolate frame vibrations
uav-imu.li2_5: Software filtering: Digital filtering algorithms to remove vibration interference
uav-imu.li2_6: Vibration resistance spec: PA-IMU-01D can withstand 5g vibration (10Hz~500Hz)
uav-imu.p2_3: For flight control applications requiring real-time response:
uav-imu.li2_8: Data latency: As low as possible, typically <2ms required
uav-imu.li2_9: Output protocol: SPI, UART, CAN commonly used
uav-imu.p2_4: Requirements for bias stability vary with flight mission:
uav-imu.p3_1: Multi-rotor UAVs are the most common type with special IMU requirements:
uav-imu.li3_1: Long hover time, relatively gentle attitude changes
uav-imu.li3_2: High motor vibration, requires good vibration damping
uav-imu.li3_3: Size and weight sensitive, requires compact lightweight IMU
uav-imu.li3_4: Cost sensitive, especially for consumer grade
uav-imu.p3_2: For multi-rotor UAVs, PA-IMU-01D is recommended:
uav-imu.li3_5: Gyro bias: <0.5°/h, meets most multi-rotor requirements
uav-imu.li3_6: Size: Compact design, easy to install on flight control board
uav-imu.li3_7: Interface: Supports RS-422/RS-232, compatible with mainstream flight controls
uav-imu.li3_8: Power consumption: Low power design, suitable for battery powered
uav-imu.li3_9: Use shock pads to isolate motor vibrations
uav-imu.li3_10: Install near the center of gravity
uav-imu.li3_11: Ensure IMU orientation matches flight control coordinate system
uav-imu.p4_1: Fixed-wing UAVs have unique flight characteristics:
uav-imu.li4_1: Long cruise time, relatively stable attitude
uav-imu.li4_2: May require larger measurement range for high-speed flight
uav-imu.li4_3: Surveying applications have higher precision requirements
uav-imu.li4_4: Very small angular velocity during gliding, requires low noise
uav-imu.reason2: Requires low drift for long endurance
uav-imu.p4_3: Surveying fixed-wing recommends PA-IMU-01G, high-precision version meets professional surveying requirements.
uav-imu.p5_1: Unmanned helicopters differ in structure from multi-rotors, with corresponding IMU selection differences:
uav-imu.li5_1: Main rotor vibration: Complex frequency, harder to suppress than multi-rotor
uav-imu.li5_2: High maneuverability: Acrobatic flight may require ±2000°/s measurement range
uav-imu.li5_3: Gyroscopic effect: Requires high-speed sampling to prevent rotor precession interference
uav-imu.p5_2: Unmanned helicopters recommend: High bandwidth, high sampling rate IMU like PA-IMU-01D with excellent vibration damping performance.
uav-imu.h2_6: 6. Comparison of Mainstream UAV Flight Control IMU Solutions
uav-imu.faq1_q: Q1: Must multi-rotor UAVs use industrial-grade IMU?
uav-imu.faq1_a: Not necessarily. Consumer multi-rotors can use consumer-grade MEMS IMU, but if you need more stable flight performance, better hover precision, or longer endurance autonomous navigation, industrial-grade IMU like PA-IMU-01D is recommended. Industrial IMU has lower drift and better temperature stability.
uav-imu.faq2_q: Q2: Is higher IMU sampling rate always better?
uav-imu.faq2_a: Not absolutely. Sampling rate needs to match flight control processor capability. For most flight controls, 400Hz-1kHz raw data sampling is sufficient. Higher sampling rates increase processor burden and may cause data overflow.
uav-imu.faq3_q: Q3: How to reduce UAV vibration impact on IMU?
uav-imu.faq3_a: Consider: 1) Use soft shock pads for vibration isolation; 2) Ensure motor balance to reduce vibration source; 3) Choose IMU with good vibration resistance; 4) Use software filtering (like low-pass filter) to remove vibration noise.
uav-imu.faq4_q: Q4: What is the difference between UAV flight control IMU and regular IMU?
uav-imu.faq4_a: UAV flight control IMU is optimized for UAV applications: 1) Higher sampling rate and lower latency; 2) Better vibration damping; 3) Wider operating temperature range; 4) Usually integrated on flight control board, directly outputs attitude data.
uav-imu.summary: Selecting the right UAV IMU requires considering aircraft type, application scenario, precision requirements and budget. PA-IMU series provides complete product line from consumer to industrial grade, meeting various UAV needs.
uav-imu.contact: For further selection consultation, please contact our technical support team.
uav-imu.product1Desc: MEMS IMU, suitable for multi-rotor and fixed-wing UAVs
uav-imu.product2Desc: AHRS with magnetometer for absolute heading
uav-imu.cardTitle: UAV IMU Selection Guide: Choosing the Right Inertial Measurement Unit for Aircraft
uav-imu.cardExcerpt: Detailed introduction to key IMU parameters for multi-rotor, fixed-wing, and helicopter flight control, helping you select the best inertial measurement unit for your aircraft.

laser-gyro.title: Laser Gyro vs MEMS IMU Comparison: How to Choose the Right Inertial Navigation Solution | MEMS-MCTI Tech Blog
laser-gyro.description: Laser gyro vs MEMS IMU comparison: Detailed analysis of technical principles, accuracy performance, size, power consumption and selection decision for FOG and MEMS IMU
laser-gyro.keywords: Laser gyro, FOG, fiber optic gyro, MEMS IMU, accuracy comparison, inertial navigation selection
laser-gyro.og_title: Laser Gyro vs MEMS IMU Comparison: How to Choose the Right Inertial Navigation Solution
laser-gyro.og_description: Detailed analysis of technical principles, accuracy performance and selection decision for FOG and MEMS IMU
laser-gyro.h1: Laser Gyro vs MEMS IMU Comparison: How to Choose the Right Inertial Navigation Solution
laser-gyro.intro: When selecting an inertial navigation solution, engineers often face the choice between Ring Laser Gyro (RLG), Fiber Optic Gyro (FOG), and MEMS IMU. This article provides a comprehensive comparison from technical principles, accuracy performance, size, weight, power consumption and cost dimensions to help you make the best choice based on specific application scenarios.
laser-gyro.p1_1: Laser gyros are based on the Sagnac Effect, measuring angular velocity through interference of two laser beams propagating in opposite directions in a closed optical path:
laser-gyro.li1_2: Measurement principle: Optical interference, frequency difference proportional to angular velocity
laser-gyro.li1_4: Representative products: Honeywell HG1900, Honeywell GG1308
laser-gyro.p1_2: FOG is also based on Sagnac Effect, but uses fiber optic coil as the propagation medium:
laser-gyro.li1_5: Working medium: Fiber optic coil (typically hundreds to thousands of meters)
laser-gyro.li1_6: Measurement principle: Interference phase change proportional to angular velocity
laser-gyro.p1_3: MEMS (Micro-Electro-Mechanical Systems) gyroscopes utilize the Coriolis Force effect:
laser-gyro.li1_9: Working principle: Coriolis force generated by vibrating mass during rotation
laser-gyro.li1_10: Manufacturing process: Semiconductor micro-machining, silicon-based materials
laser-gyro.p2_1: Bias stability is the core indicator of gyroscope performance:
laser-gyro.p2_2: ARW characterizes gyroscope short-term noise performance:
laser-gyro.p2_3: Scale factor stability determines full-range measurement accuracy:
laser-gyro.li2_5: Laser gyro: <1 ppm, excellent long-term stability
laser-gyro.li2_7: MEMS: 100 ppm ~ 1000 ppm, significantly affected by temperature
laser-gyro.p3_1: Size and weight are key considerations for portable applications:
laser-gyro.li3_1: Laser gyro: 5W ~ 30W (requires high voltage power for laser excitation)
laser-gyro.p5_1: Aerospace has extremely high requirements for precision and reliability:
laser-gyro.li4_1: Commercial aircraft: Laser gyro INS combined with GPS for redundant navigation
laser-gyro.li4_2: Military aircraft: Laser gyro/FOG, high anti-interference capability
laser-gyro.li4_3: Launch vehicles: FOG, capable of withstanding high overload
laser-gyro.p5_2: Industrial applications require balancing precision, cost and reliability:
laser-gyro.li4_4: Geodetic surveying: FOG, high-precision static measurement
laser-gyro.li4_5: Oil drilling: MEMS or FOG, withstand harsh environments
laser-gyro.li4_6: Bridge health monitoring: MEMS, low noise, high resolution
laser-gyro.li4_7: Autonomous driving: MEMS, high sampling rate, low latency
laser-gyro.p5_3: Consumer electronics pursue low cost and small size:
laser-gyro.li4_10: AR/VR devices: MEMS, high sampling rate tracking
laser-gyro.p6_1: The following questions can help you quickly select the appropriate technology:
laser-gyro.h3_13: Step 1: What are the precision requirements?
laser-gyro.q1_a: If precision <0.01°/h required: Choose laser gyro
laser-gyro.q1_b: If precision 0.01°/h ~ 1°/h: Choose FOG or high-precision MEMS
laser-gyro.q2_a: If extremely small size required (<10cm³): MEMS is the only choice
laser-gyro.q2_b: If medium size acceptable: MEMS, FOG or small RLG available
laser-gyro.q3_a: If battery powered with long endurance required: MEMS is the best choice
laser-gyro.q3_b: If stable power available: Select based on precision requirements
laser-gyro.q4_a: If budget sufficient (>$5000): Laser gyro or FOG
laser-gyro.q4_b: If budget limited: MEMS industrial solution
laser-gyro.h3_17: Case 1: Commercial Aircraft Inertial Navigation System
laser-gyro.case1: Uses laser gyro inertial navigation system, redundant navigation with GPS, typical precision <0.01°/h, system cost approximately $30,000-$50,000.
laser-gyro.case2: Uses MEMS IMU (PA-IMU-01D), bias <0.5°/h, power <0.3W, highly integrated with flight control board.
laser-gyro.case3: Uses FOG (PA-IMU-02D03D), bias <0.01°/h, precision meets 1:500 surveying requirements.
laser-gyro.case4: Uses high-precision MEMS IMU, compact design, supports Bluetooth communication, suitable for field measurement operations.
laser-gyro.summary: Choosing between laser gyro and MEMS IMU requires comprehensive consideration of precision requirements, size constraints, power constraints and budget based on specific application scenarios. PA series inertial products cover complete technology from MEMS to FOG, meeting various application needs in aerospace, industrial control, UAV and other fields.
laser-gyro.contact: Welcome to contact our technical support team for professional selection advice.
laser-gyro.product1Desc: Laser strapdown inertial navigation system, navigation-grade precision
laser-gyro.faq1_q: Q1: What is the difference between laser gyro and fiber optic gyro?
laser-gyro.faq1_a: Laser gyros use helium-neon gas lasers to produce coherent light, while fiber optic gyros use fiber coils. Laser gyros have higher precision but larger size and higher cost; fiber optic gyros are smaller and suitable for medium precision requirements.
laser-gyro.faq2_q: Q2: Can MEMS IMU meet industrial application requirements?
laser-gyro.faq2_a: Modern MEMS IMU can meet most industrial application requirements. Industrial-grade MEMS products like PA-IMU-01D provide 0.5°/h bias stability, suitable for industrial UAVs, vehicle navigation, robotics and other fields.
laser-gyro.faq3_q: Q3: How to select the appropriate inertial navigation solution based on application?
laser-gyro.faq3_a: Key considerations include: 1) Precision requirements; 2) Size and weight constraints; 3) Power constraints; 4) Budget; 5) Environmental conditions. Recommended to first determine precision range, then filter appropriate technology type based on other constraints.
laser-gyro.faq4_q: Q4: What are the advantages of PA series products?
laser-gyro.faq4_a: PA series inertial products have complete technology coverage from MEMS to FOG, providing full-range products from consumer to navigation grade, meeting different application needs, with local technical support.
laser-gyro.cardTitle: Laser Gyro vs MEMS IMU: How to Choose the Right Inertial Navigation Solution
laser-gyro.cardExcerpt: Comprehensive analysis of FOG, laser strapdown INS, and MEMS IMU: technical principles, accuracy, size, power consumption, and selection decision-making.

ahrs-application.title: AHRS Application Guide | MEMS-MCTI Tech Blog
ahrs-application.description: AHRS Application Guide: Detailed explanation of IMU+magnetometer+GPS fusion algorithms, selection for UAV, vehicle navigation, maritime attitude measurement applications
ahrs-application.keywords: AHRS selection, attitude measurement, heading reference, UAV attitude, vehicle navigation, MEMS sensors
ahrs-application.og_description: Detailed explanation of IMU+magnetometer+GPS fusion algorithms, selection for UAV, vehicle navigation, maritime attitude measurement applications
ahrs-application.intro: AHRS (Attitude and Heading Reference System) is an important attitude measurement device that fuses IMU data with magnetometer and/or GPS data to provide comprehensive attitude and heading information. This article details AHRS working principles, differences from IMU, industry applications and selection key points.
ahrs-application.p1_1: A typical AHRS system includes the following sensors:
ahrs-application.li1_1: Three-axis gyroscope: Measures angular velocity, provides dynamic attitude change information
ahrs-application.li1_2: Three-axis accelerometer: Measures gravitational acceleration, provides static attitude reference
ahrs-application.li1_3: Three-axis magnetometer: Measures Earth magnetic field, provides absolute heading reference
ahrs-application.li1_4: Embedded processor: Runs data fusion algorithms, outputs attitude quaternions or Euler angles
ahrs-application.p1_2: AHRS uses advanced data fusion algorithms to fuse data from different sensors:
ahrs-application.p1_3: EKF is the most commonly used fusion algorithm in AHRS:
ahrs-application.li1_5: State prediction: Predict attitude using gyroscope integration
ahrs-application.li1_6: Measurement update: Correct attitude errors using accelerometer and magnetometer
ahrs-application.li1_7: Adaptive weighting: Adjust sensor weights based on dynamic state
ahrs-application.p1_4: Uses quaternions to represent attitude, avoiding gimbal lock of Euler angles:
ahrs-application.li1_8: Quaternion q = [qw, qx, qy, qz] represents rotation
ahrs-application.li1_10: High computational efficiency, suitable for embedded systems
ahrs-application.p1_5: AHRS systems typically output the following data:
ahrs-application.outDesc3: Rotation around Z-axis relative to magnetic north
ahrs-application.p2_1: IMU and AHRS play different roles in systems:
ahrs-application.p2_2: Choosing IMU or AHRS depends on application requirements:
ahrs-application.li2_1: If only raw sensor data needed for flight control processing: Choose IMU
ahrs-application.li2_2: If direct attitude angle output needed for display or control: Choose AHRS
ahrs-application.li2_3: If absolute heading reference needed: Must choose AHRS
ahrs-application.li2_4: If system already has mature flight control algorithm: IMU is more flexible
ahrs-application.p3_1: AHRS provides key attitude information for UAVs:
ahrs-application.li3_1: Flight status display: Cockpit shows actual Roll, Pitch, Yaw angles
ahrs-application.li3_2: Autonomous control: Provides attitude reference for auto-return, hover stabilization
ahrs-application.li3_3: Safety protection: Detects abnormal attitude to trigger protection mechanisms
ahrs-application.p3_2: Absolute heading from magnetometer enables UAVs to:
ahrs-application.li3_6: Achieve automatic takeoff and landing orientation
ahrs-application.p3_3: Airborne gimbals use AHRS data for attitude stabilization:
ahrs-application.li3_8: Keep camera stable relative to ground
ahrs-application.p4_1: AHRS is an important component of vehicle navigation systems:
ahrs-application.li4_1: Tunnel positioning: Provides dead reckoning when GPS signal is lost
ahrs-application.li4_2: Slope measurement: Detects road longitudinal slope for dynamics control
ahrs-application.li4_3: Body stability control: Provides attitude data for ESC systems
ahrs-application.p4_2: AHRS plays key role in ADAS:
ahrs-application.h2_5: 5. AHRS Applications in Maritime Attitude Measurement
ahrs-application.p5_1: AHRS provides attitude monitoring for ships:
ahrs-application.li5_1: Roll/pitch monitoring: Real-time display of ship attitude
ahrs-application.li5_2: Stabilizer fin control: Provides feedback signal for stabilization system
ahrs-application.li5_3: Deck operation reference: Ensures safe operation angle range
ahrs-application.p5_2: High-precision AHRS used for marine engineering:
ahrs-application.li5_4: Underwater topographic surveying: Attitude compensation improves measurement accuracy
ahrs-application.li5_5: Drilling platform positioning: Monitor platform stability
ahrs-application.li5_6: Offshore operation support: Helicopter landing reference
ahrs-application.p6_1: This is the most important selection parameter:
ahrs-application.p6_2: Select based on system integration requirements:
ahrs-application.li6_2: CAN bus: Automotive and maritime industry standard
ahrs-application.li6_9: Protection rating: IP67 for maritime outdoor applications
ahrs-application.p7_1: PA-AHRS01 is an industrial-grade AHRS product launched by MEMS-MCTI:
ahrs-application.faq1_q: Q1: What is the difference between AHRS and GPS-INS?
ahrs-application.faq1_a: AHRS provides attitude and heading reference but does not include position information. GPS-INS integrates GPS receiver on AHRS basis, can output position (latitude, longitude, altitude) and velocity information. For applications that need to know "where" position, choose GPS-INS or combine AHRS with GPS.
ahrs-application.faq2_q: Q2: What factors affect AHRS heading precision?
ahrs-application.faq2_a: Heading precision is mainly affected by magnetometer accuracy and local magnetic field environment. Hard magnetic interference (nearby ferromagnetic materials) and soft magnetic interference (geomagnetic field distortion) both reduce heading precision. PA-AHRS01 supports dual-antenna GPS-aided heading, achieving <0.1° heading precision.
ahrs-application.faq3_q: Q3: How to calibrate AHRS magnetometer?
ahrs-application.faq3_a: Magnetometer calibration typically includes: 1) Ellipsoid calibration, compensate sensor sensitivity differences; 2) Hard iron compensation, offset fixed magnetic interference; 3) Soft iron compensation, offset environmental magnetic field distortion. PA-AHRS01 supports automatic calibration mode, completing calibration by rotating 2-3 circles in open area.
ahrs-application.faq4_q: Q4: Will AHRS drift under dynamic conditions?
ahrs-application.faq4_a: AHRS attitude angles will still have some drift under dynamic conditions, caused by gyroscope integration error accumulation. Drift can be suppressed through data fusion with accelerometer and magnetometer. For long-term high-precision requirements, GPS-INS integrated navigation system is recommended.
ahrs-application.summary: As an important attitude heading reference system, AHRS is widely used in UAVs, vehicle navigation, maritime attitude measurement and other fields. Selecting the right AHRS requires considering precision requirements, communication interfaces, environmental adaptability and cost factors. PA-AHRS01 with its excellent performance and reliability is an ideal choice for industrial-grade applications.
ahrs-application.contact: Welcome to contact our technical support team for professional selection advice.
ahrs-application.product1Desc: Attitude heading reference system, industrial-grade precision
ahrs-application.product2Desc: Positioning and attitude system, dual-antenna heading support
ahrs-application.cardTitle: AHRS Application Guide: Attitude Heading Reference System
ahrs-application.cardExcerpt: Detailed explanation of IMU+magnetometer+GPS fusion algorithms, UAV, vehicle navigation, and maritime attitude measurement application selection points.
